content:
3 min read
Just now

--

I was working on my laptop a few days ago when a notification came through on my phone.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Someone needed a volunteer writer for Campus Launchpad — a seven-week programme set up for secondary school graduates, admission seekers, and freshers to help them navigate one of the biggest life transitions they'll ever face: starting university. Because campus life isn't just about passing exams.
I let that sink for a moment.
I knew I wasn’t going to get paid. But something else caught my attention, saying yes meant writing copy for a real brand for the first time. Copy that would actually be used. Copy that would go out into the world on someone else’s platforms, representing something real. And since I’m actively working to make my copywriting skills solid, I saw it for exactly what it was: an opportunity to grow. So I accepted.
The Moment It Felt Real
After agreeing to volunteer, I was added to a group with the other volunteers. Shortly after, I received a flier and was asked to write a social media caption copy for the programme before it went live across their platforms.
That moment, writing copy for a brand that actually exists, knowing someone was going to post it publicly felt entirely different from everything I’d written before. Spec work is valuable practice. But this was real. A real brand. A real audience. A real purpose behind every word.
I was thrilled. I knew exactly what this felt like now, writing something for someone who was actually going to use it.
So I got into it. I wrote the draft, edited it, refined it, and sent it over. She asked me to also write a shorter version for WhatsApp status but this was the original caption I wrote, and it’s the one I’m most proud of.
🎓 Your Campus Life Begins Here
University isn't just about passing exams. It's about discovering who you are while navigating one of the biggest transitions of your life.
The problem? Most students are thrown into campus life without the skills, mindset, or guidance to handle it.
That's where Campus Launchpad helps you get ahead.
Campus Launchpad is a 7-week transformational programme designed for secondary school students, fresh undergraduates, and recent graduates.
In this programme, you'll:
✓ Understand yourself better and gain clarity about your future
✓ Build habits and systems that help you stay focused and productive
✓ Develop valuable digital skills for today's world
✓ Learn how to handle pressure, challenges, and opportunities with confidence
Open to:
• SS2 Students
• SS3 Students
• Recent Secondary School Graduates
• First-Year University Students
Spaces are limited.
Register now: bit.ly/Campuslaunchpad
Tag a student, parent, or guardian who needs to see this.
She went through it and said it was “perfect” and even expressed an heartfelt gratitude
A real feedback. From a real brand.
What That Moment Did to Me
Something quietly shifted when I read that response.
I’m not just a copywriter who writes spec work anymore. I’m a copywriter who writes for real people, real brands and whose words actually get used. That distinction matters more than I expected it to. It gave me a quiet confidence I hadn’t felt before, the kind that doesn’t come from telling yourself you’re good but from having someone else confirm it with their actions.
I am convinced I’m stepping onto higher ground with this skill. And I’m genuinely grateful to God for the gift He has placed in me and the path He’s been walking me through even the hard parts of it.
-
Whatever Stage You’re At, Don’t Stop
If you’re in the middle of building a skill right now , be it writing, design, coding, anything, promise yourself this: stay consistent. Keep loving what you do.
Giving up because you’re not getting noticed, or because you think you’re not good enough yet, won’t make it any better. Every successful creator you admire went through the same quiet, unrewarded beginning. The difference between those who made it and those who didn’t isn’t talent. It’s the decision to keep going in the face of opposition, discouragement and pressure, until everything eventually becomes sweet and glamorous.
Stay true to yourself. Keep doing what you're doing.
It's only a matter of time.
